page: Microsoft Azure D48d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions and Intel Deep Learning Boost (AVX-512 VNNI). The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1843 GB of bundled local storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the D48d_v4 in the middle tier for most compute and memory operations. The instance achieves a strong Passmark memory score, supported by a read-only cached memory bandwidth of 2492.11 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth of 70.1 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is solid, with a Passmark CPU score of 35075.75 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 67.78 GB/sec. However, text generation speeds for large language models are relatively weak, yielding 44.98 tokens/sec for a 135M model and dropping to 7.48 tokens/sec for a 7B model, indicating a clear tradeoff for real-time AI inference workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48d_v4 offers a balanced resource profile, combining high-capacity local storage with a standard memory-to-core ratio. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Recommended workloads include relational databases, static web serving (which achieved 27.62 GB/sec in throughput tests), Redis caching layers (processing 40.98 million SET operations per second), and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of bundled local storage, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show strong memory capabilities, highlighted by a top-tier Passmark memory score and a cached read bandwidth of 2492.11 GB/sec. While multi-core CPU performance is solid, with a Passmark CPU score of 35075.75, the instance exhibits weaker performance in LLM text generation tasks. Qualitatively, the server provides balanced resource density and cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. It is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, optimized for databases, web serving, and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine combining 48 Intel vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and strong memory performance for enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPUs at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of bundled local storage
Strong memory performance with high-speed cached read bandwidth
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
Weaker performance for real-time large language model text generation
